artPrompt: A characterful portrait of a sodden goblin spirit rising dripping from black reservoir water at the foot of a rain-slick dock, weeds tangled in his collar, a waterlogged clipboard clutched proudly in one clawed hand and a smug ghostly grin on his face. Amber dock lamplight ripples across the surface, mist coiling upward. Mono-black noir of ink shadow and smoky brown, eerie undrowned menace, composed wide in a 4:3 landscape frame, rendered as a vibrant gouache storybook illustration — saturated matte color, simplified flat-ish shapes, soft painterly edges, decorative.
name: Loanshark Larry, Last Seen Underwater
text:
Larry can't be blocked except by creatures that owe him a Favor, and creatures can't owe Larry a Favor, so Larry can't be blocked. (He worked this out himself. He's very proud.)
Whenever Larry deals combat damage to a player, that player owes you a Favor from beyond the grave (create a Favor token). Death did not settle Larry's accounts. Death made the deadlines worse.
When Larry dies, return him to the battlefield at the beginning of your next upkeep. He still hasn't been paid. He is not leaving until he is.
flavorText: They put him in the reservoir. He swam back up with a clipboard.
